summary: Rare excellent soundboard of a mixed‑quality 1995 show with standout Stella Blue outro and solid Alabama Getaway, though the band sounds a little off.
review: The SBD capture is praised for its crisp mix, vibrant vocals and Vince’s spirited piano, delivering an unusually clear snapshot of a 1995 Dead concert. Musically the night has high points – a powerful Alabama Getaway opener, an inspiring Stella Blue guitar outro and generous Space jams – but several tunes (Unbroken Chain, Crazy Fingers, Corinna) feel uneven and the overall band tightness is described as slightly off.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers highlight a high‑quality soundboard and several strong performances, while only a few note uneven playing; the weighted average of detailed comments yields an overall rating around four stars.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Great mix, crisp clarity, vocals and piano stand out; rare high‑fidelity SBD for a 1995 show. Minor performance flaws noted but they do not detract from the recording itself.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is short (six songs) and ends with the first‑ever performance of a song, giving it a historic hook. The second set offers unique moments, generous Space jams and an emotionally resonant close, though the overall band feels slightly off compared to other ’95 dates.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Pulls out hot licks and solid solos (Stella Blue outro) but has a few flubs (Crazy Fingers, teleprompter lag) and sounds less dominant than usual.
band_member_comments.Bob: Wonderful vocal performance noted; briefly cuts off Jerry on Samson.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince’s piano described as spirited and adds to the mix's richness.
band_member_comments.Drums: Long, soaring Space section typical of ’95, highlighted as a high point.
